meta.title: AI Exposure of the Canadian Job Market
meta.primary_rollup_note: The dashboard rolls canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through the 45 official major groups.
meta.metric_model_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables are published at a coarser occupation grain than official NOC 2021 unit groups, so the unit-group labour metrics are allocated using ESDC unit employment weights. Outlook remains direct from the ESDC unit-group release.
meta.exposure_metric_scale: 0-10 display score derived from the share of workers in high-exposure EPIAC occupations
meta.exposure_note: Official EPIAC fields are mapped from published StatCan occupation groups onto the canonical NOC 2021 unit groups.
meta.profile_layer_note: Official OaSIS occupational profiles attach to canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through an explicit generated mapping table.
meta.source: Statistics Canada annual occupation tables rebuilt onto the canonical NOC 2021 spine

oasis.title: Occupational and Skills Information System (OaSIS) - 2025 Version 1.0

family.title: Assisting occupations in education and in legal and public protection
family.category: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
family.definition: This major group comprises assisting occupations in education and in legal and public protection. These occupations come with Training, Education, Experience and Responsibility (TEER) identified as TEER 3. Occupations classified in TEER 3, as those included in this group with a 3 as the second digit of their code, typically require completion of a post-secondary education program of less than two years at community college, institute of technology or CÉGEP; or completion of an apprenticeship training program of less than two years; or more than six months of on-the-job training, training courses or specific work experience with some secondary school education; or the accumulation of several years of experience in a related occupation found in TEER 4, when applicable (TEER 4 includes occupations with a 4 as a second digit in their code).
family.broad_category_title: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
family.major_group_title: Assisting occupations in education and in legal and public protection
family.exposure_rationale: Aggregated from canonical unit groups. EPIAC values roll up unit-level mappings from published StatCan occupation groups.
family.metric_rationale: This major group aggregates canonical unit groups. Labour-market counts and wages reflect the unit-group estimates allocated from published StatCan annual occupation tables using ESDC employment weights.
family.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Support occupations in law and social services (42, 43, 44)

units.title: Elementary and secondary school teacher assistants
units.category: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
units.broad_category_title: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
units.major_group_title: Assisting occupations in education and in legal and public protection
units.definition: Elementary and secondary school teacher assistants support students, and assist teachers and counsellors with teaching and non-instructional tasks. They assist in areas of personal care, teaching and behaviour management under the supervision of teachers or other child care professionals. They are employed in public and private elementary, secondary and special needs schools and treatment centres.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Support occupations in law and social services (42, 43, 44).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Support occupations in law and social services (42, 43, 44)

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Elementary and secondary school teacher assistants
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Elementary and secondary school teacher assistants
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Elementary and secondary school teacher assistants support students, and assist teachers and counsellors with teaching and non-instructional tasks. They assist in areas of personal care, teaching and behaviour management under the supervision of teachers or other child care professionals.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Teacher assistants usually require completion of a 10-month college program in teaching assistance, educational assistance or other social science program.
Teacher assistants who help students with special needs usually require specialized training and experience.
additional_information: Mobility to other occupations related to child care and adolescent supportive instruction is possible with experience.

example_titles.job_title_text: teaching staff assistant - primary and secondary school
instructional assistant - primary and secondary school
classroom assistant - primary and secondary school

core_competencies.competency: Learning and Teaching Strategies, and Instructing
core_competencies.statement: to implement and reinforce teaching strategies designed by the teacher
to be aware of students’ social interactions, behaviour and non-verbal cues to manage classroom dynamics and provide support where needed
to focus on and interpret verbal instructions, student queries and classroom discussions
to clarify instructions, provide constructive feedback and communicate with students and teachers in a clear and accessible manner

core_competencies.competency: Concern for Others and Social orientation
core_competencies.statement: to respond to students' emotional and educational needs to foster a supportive and inclusive learning environment

to provide support for students and teachers, aligning  assistance with the educational goals established by the educators, providing constructive feedback and facilitating the development of tailored learning plans

units.title: Other instructors
units.category: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
units.broad_category_title: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
units.major_group_title: Assisting occupations in education and in legal and public protection
units.definition: Other instructors teach courses, such as motor vehicle or motorcycle driving, sailing and navigation, sewing or other courses, which are outside of educational institutions. They are employed by driving schools, fabric retailers and other commercial establishments or they may be self-employed. This unit group also includes modelling and finishing school instructors, driver's licence examiners, who are employed by provincial governments and tutors who provide instruction in elementary or secondary school subjects.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

epiac_source_groups: Support occupations in law and social services (42, 43, 44)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Driving instructors teach motor vehicle and motorcycle driving courses that are outside of educational institutions.
main_duties: Instruct individuals on proper motor vehicle driving skills and traffic regulations
Demonstrate and explain the handling and mechanical operation of motor vehicles
Supervise individuals during practice driving.
employment_requirements: Expertise in the field of instruction must be demonstrated.
Driving instructors must obtain special licensing as a driving instructor in the province of employment.
Certification by the Canadian Yachting Association may be required for sailing and navigation instructors.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Driver's licence examiners conduct road tests and evaluate driving ability of individuals.
main_duties: Conduct road test examinations
Evaluate the driving ability of applicants for driver's licences.
employment_requirements: Expertise in the field of instruction must be demonstrated.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Sewing instructors teach sewing courses that are outside of educational institutions.
main_duties: Give instruction to students in techniques and skills of sewing, tailoring and dressmaking.
employment_requirements: Expertise in the field of instruction must be demonstrated.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Modelling instructors teach modelling courses that are outside of educational institutions.
main_duties: Give instruction on modelling techniques for fashion shows and magazine advertising.
employment_requirements: Expertise in the field of instruction must be demonstrated.

units.title: Sheriffs and bailiffs
units.category: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
units.broad_category_title: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
units.major_group_title: Assisting occupations in education and in legal and public protection
units.sub_major_group_title: Assisting occupations in legal and public protection
units.minor_group_title: Assisting occupations in legal and public protection
units.definition: Sheriffs execute and enforce court orders, warrants and writs, participate in seizure and sale of property and perform courtroom and other related duties. Bailiffs serve legal orders and documents, seize or repossess properties, evict tenants and perform other related activities. Sheriffs and bailiffs must usually complete a training program offered by the province to work in the provincial courts. They are employed by federal, provincial, territorial and municipal courts, and bailiffs may be employed as officers of the court or in private service as agents for creditors.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Support occupations in law and social services (42, 43, 44)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Sheriffs execute and enforce court orders, warrants and writs, participate in seizure and sale of property and perform courtroom and other related duties. Bailiffs serve legal orders and documents, seize or repossess properties, evict tenants and perform other related activities. Sheriffs and bailiffs must usually complete a training program offered by the province to work in the provincial courts.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
A college diploma or bachelor's degree in legal studies is usually required.
Sheriffs require experience as a deputy sheriff or bailiff and/or in custodial/escort or security work.
Bailiffs usually require some work experience related to law enforcement as well as knowledge of relevant statutes and laws.
Mandatory provincial training may be required.
Membership in the provincial regulatory body is mandatory in Quebec.
additional_information: Sheriffs and bailiffs may also serve as court officers (14103), depending on the location and size of the courthouse.
Progression to supervisory positions is possible with additional experience and in-house training.

core_competencies.competency: Critical Thinking, Decision Making and Problem Solving
core_competencies.statement: to make informed decisions and solve problems as they arise, such as assessing the legality of actions during property seizures or determining the best course of action during an eviction
to adequately manage conflicts, de-escalate tense situations and communicate effectively with people from diverse backgrounds
to handle physically demanding situations, such as evicting tenants or securing property during seizures
to quickly respond during the enforcement of court orders or in scenarios where immediate action is necessary
to remain calm and effective in high-pressure situations, such as confrontations, disputes, or complex legal scenarios
to understand the actions necessary to be compliant with laws and protects stations from legal challenges, including execution of court orders, property seizures or evictions

units.title: Correctional service officers
units.category: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
units.broad_category_title: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
units.major_group_title: Assisting occupations in education and in legal and public protection
units.sub_major_group_title: Assisting occupations in legal and public protection
units.minor_group_title: Assisting occupations in legal and public protection
units.definition: Correctional service officers guard offenders and detainees and maintain order in correctional institutions and other places of detention. Primary workers develop women offender's correctional plan and work with other service officers in the correctional team. They are employed by federal, provincial and municipal governments. Correctional service officers who are supervisors are included in this unit group.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

epiac_source_groups: Support occupations in law and social services (42, 43, 44)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Correctional service officers guard offenders and detainees and maintain order in correctional institutions and other places of detention. Primary workers develop women offender’s correctional plan and work with other service officers in the correctional team. Correctional service officers who are supervisors are included in this unit group.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Post-secondary education in correctional services, police studies or criminology is required in New Brunswick, Quebec, and Alberta and Prince Edward Island.
Correctional officer recruits must successfully complete the Correctional Service of Canada training course to be employed by federal institutions.
Correctional officer recruits are usually required to complete a basic training course to be employed by provincial/territorial institutions.
Physical agility, strength and fitness requirements must be met.
Correctional service supervisors require experience as a correctional service officer.
First aid certification and cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) training are usually required.
additional_information: Primary workers are the primary daily contact for women offenders.

core_competencies.statement: to de-escalate volatile situations and manage conflicts which may occur between inmates or other officers
to gauge the mood of inmates, anticipate potential issues and interact more effectively with both inmates and colleagues
to act swiftly to control the situation, restrain individuals or prevent harm in violent or emergency scenarios
to monitor surroundings while focusing ahead, detecting movements or potential threats from the sides without turning their head
to make sound decisions in a highly stressful environment to reduce conflict and maintain security
to understand inmate control practices and protocols to ensure inmates, employees and officers are protected and threats are prevented

units.category: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
units.broad_category_title: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services

units.sub_major_group_title: Assisting occupations in legal and public protection
units.minor_group_title: Assisting occupations in legal and public protection
units.definition: By-law enforcement and other regulatory officers enforce by-laws and regulations of provincial and municipal governments. They are employed by provincial and municipal governments and agencies.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: By-law enforcement and other regulatory officers
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Animal control officers enforce by-laws and regulations of provincial and municipal governments concerning stray domestic animals, livestock and wildlife.
main_duties: Respond to citizen complaints concerning stray domestic animals, livestock and wildlife
Issue warnings and citations to owners
Impound lost, homeless and dangerous animals.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Completion of a college program or courses in law and security or other related field orexperience in a related administrative or regulatory occupation is usually required.
On-the-job training is provided.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: By-law enforcement and other regulatory officers
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: By-law enforcement officers enforce municipal and provincial regulations.
main_duties: Enforce municipal and provincial regulations
Investigate complaints
Issue warnings and citations to commercial and residential property owners and occupants.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Completion of a college program or courses in law and security or other related field orexperience in a related administrative or regulatory occupation is usually required.
On-the-job training is provided.
additional_information: By-law enforcement officers may specialize in the enforcement of one specific by-law.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: By-law enforcement and other regulatory officers
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Commercial transport inspectors ensure compliance with regulations governing load restrictions, the transportation of hazardous materials and public safety.
main_duties: Inspect commercial vehicles to ensure compliance with regulations governing load restrictions, the transportation of hazardous materials and public safety.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Completion of a college program or courses in law and security or other related field orexperience in a related administrative or regulatory occupation is usually required.
On-the-job training is provided.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: By-law enforcement and other regulatory officers
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Garbage collection inspectors investigate complaints concerning infractions of garbage collection by-laws.
main_duties: Investigate complaints concerning infractions of garbage collection by-laws.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Completion of a college program or courses in law and security or other related field orexperience in a related administrative or regulatory occupation is usually required.
On-the-job training is provided.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: By-law enforcement and other regulatory officers
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Liquor licence inspectors enforce provincial liquor laws.
main_duties: Conduct inspections of licensed establishments
Advise licensees on laws and regulations
Report contravention of laws and regulations to provincial liquor control boards and agencies.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Completion of a college program or courses in law and security or other related field orexperience in a related administrative or regulatory occupation is usually required.
On-the-job training is provided.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: By-law enforcement and other regulatory officers
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Parking control officers enforce by-laws and regulations of provincial and municipal governments concerning parking by-laws on city streets, regional roads and municipal properties.
main_duties: Enforce parking by-laws on city streets, regional roads and municipal properties.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Completion of a college program or courses in law and security or other related field orexperience in a related administrative or regulatory occupation is usually required.
On-the-job training is provided.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: By-law enforcement and other regulatory officers
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Taxi inspectors inspect taxicabs for mechanical reliability, cleanliness, licensing and meter accuracy.
main_duties: Inspect taxicabs for mechanical reliability, cleanliness, licensing and meter accuracy
Investigate public complaints, issue citations and prepare reports
Give evidence to city councils, taxi commissions and in court.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Completion of a college program or courses in law and security or other related field orexperience in a related administrative or regulatory occupation is usually required.
On-the-job training is provided.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: By-law enforcement and other regulatory officers
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Zoning inspectors investigate properties in violation of zoning and related by-laws.
main_duties: Investigate properties in violation of zoning and related by-laws, after receiving requests for licence clearances pertaining to zoning and related by-laws, and on receipt of building permit applications.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.

Zoning inspectors or zoning enforcement officers may require a college diploma as a civil engineering or architectural technologist or technician and several years of experience in enforcement and/or building construction or a related field.
On-the-job training is provided.

units.title: Border services, customs, and immigration officers
units.category: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
units.broad_category_title: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services

units.minor_group_title: Assisting occupations in legal and public protection
units.definition: Border services, customs, and immigration officers administer and enforce laws and regulations related to immigration, customs and border crossing. They are employed by government agencies.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Border services, customs, and immigration officers
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Border services and customs officers administer and enforce laws and regulations related to customs and border crossing. They conduct interviews and inspections at border crossings to detect undeclared goods and contraband.
main_duties: Conduct interviews, using inspections and information-seeking techniques at border points as part of primary and secondary inspections for immigration, food, plant and animals, and customs program functions
Apply applicable legislation, policies, and procedure when screening and/or inspecting people and goods
Inspect baggage to detect undeclared merchandise, or contraband
Inform manufacturers and shippers of customs and laws and procedures
Observe fabrication of articles affected by customs laws and conduct appraisals
Board carriers arriving from foreign countries to determine nature of cargo to ensure compliance with customs and commerce laws
Inspect goods imported by mail
Operate firearms and use defensive tactics and physical force when necessary in the performance of duties

May conduct an arrest, detention, or conduct personal search of individuals suspected of having committed a criminal offence under the Customs Act or certain other designated Criminal Code offences until police intervention is possible.
employment_requirements: A four-week online training and a four-and-half-month specialized training at the CBSA College are required for border services officers.

Successful pass of the officer trainee entrance exam (OTEE), successful completion of the Canadian Firearms Safety Course (CGSC) and the Canadian Restricted Firearms Safety Course (CRFSC) is required for border services officers.
Must pass psychological assessment, physical abilities requirement evaluation (PARE) and medical exam to be a Border services officer.
Border services students officers must complete Control and Defensive Tactics (CDT) training.
A driver’s license is required.
additional_information: Border services officers must be willing to work anywhere in Canada, including rural and remote areas.
Progression to managerial positions in these fields is possible with experience.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Border services, customs, and immigration officers
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Immigration officers administer and enforce laws and regulations related to immigration. They determine admissibility of persons seeking entry into Canada by examining documents and conducting interviews.
main_duties: Determine admissibility of persons seeking entry into Canada by examining documents and conducting interviews
Grant landed-immigrant status, admit persons or order detention or deportation
Locate and apprehend persons presumed to be infringing on immigration laws
Assist in the removal of deported people by seeking authorization from receiving countries and securing necessary travel documents
Appear as a witness in cases related to immigration appeals.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Completion of specialized government training is usually required.
additional_information: Progression to managerial positions in these fields is possible with experience.

units.title: Operations members of the Canadian Armed Forces
units.category: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
units.broad_category_title: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
units.major_group_title: Assisting occupations in education and in legal and public protection
units.sub_major_group_title: Assisting occupations in legal and public protection
units.minor_group_title: Assisting occupations in legal and public protection
units.definition: Operations members of the Canadian Armed Forces provide operational support to soldiers including the proper functioning and testing of military equipment such as vehicles, ships and weapons. They follow the military and strategic policies established by the leadership of the Canadian Army, the Royal Canadian Airforce and the Royal Canadian Navy.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Operations Members of the Canadian Armed Forces
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Operations members of the Canadian Armed Forces
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Operations members of the Canadian Armed Forces provide operational support to soldiers including the proper functioning and testing of military equipment such as vehicles, ships and weapons. They follow the military and strategic policies established by the leadership of the Canadian Army, the Royal Canadian Airforce and the Royal Canadian Navy.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Three months of basic military training is required and provided.
Occupational qualification training of at least six months is required.
Specialized training in the operation and maintenance of military equipment and systems is provided.
additional_information: Members of this unit group who demonstrate the required abilities and potential will be offered advanced training.

core_competencies.statement: to evaluate how different systems interact within a piece of military hardware and analyze any performance issues
to oversee workload to ensure every aspect of equipment maintenance and operation is thoroughly checked and monitored in a timely manner
to ensure military operations are carried out accurately and safely, reducing the risk of errors that could compromise the equipment's functionality
to swiftly and efficiently perform repairs or adjustments during an operation
to detecting early signs of wear and tear or potential failures, including regularly checking vehicle systems, weapon performance or ship operations

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Public Safety and Security
core_competencies.statement: to identify and manage potentially hazardous materials or secure sensitive equipment to prevent accidents and protect assets
